title: Probe POR actor-hidden scale follow-up around partial_shift225 for Memory conversion

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: the new partial_shift225 point may benefit from slightly stronger hidden-FiLM scale than 0.35. Sweep scale 0.375 and 0.40 at fixed shift 0.225 against base POR and the current partial_shift225 winner. A variant only counts if it preserves greedy conversion and improves lower/gap or shoulder over the incumbent.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Commands:
- bash -n scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_scale_followup_probe.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py
- bash ./scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_scale_followup_probe.sh
- 4-GPU parallel 256-episode confirmation eval on base / partial_shift225 / partial_shift225_scale375 / partial_shift25
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q

Configs:
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_rerun.yaml
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift225.yaml
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift225_scale375.yaml
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift225_scale40.yaml

Artifacts:
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_scale_followup_probe.md
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_scale_followup_probe.json
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_scale_followup_confirmation.md
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_scale_followup_confirmation.json

Evidence:
- The 64-episode screen showed scale375 tied with partial_shift225 and scale40 fully dead.
- The 256-episode confirmation broke the tie against the scale-up: partial_shift225 kept greedy/lower/gap/shoulder 0.4688 / 0.4727 / 0.4727 / 0.4688, while scale375 stayed at 0.4688 / 0.4688 / 0.4688 / 0.4688, i.e. -0.0039 on lower and gap.
- This keeps partial_shift225 as the best-known point on the current actor-hidden FiLM surface and falsifies the idea that it wants slightly more scale.

title: Contextual route-bias architecture probe
description: Implement and validate a contextual route-bias routed SARE variant that combines recurrent route memory with a current-token-summary routing bias residual. close_reason: done: contextual route-bias architecture probe implemented, tested, and negative result verified

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Commands: ./\.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_models.py tests/test_routing.py ; bash -n scripts/run_route_bias_contextual_arch_probe.sh ; bash ./scripts/run_route_bias_contextual_arch_probe.sh ; ./\.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Configs: configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_probe.yaml ; configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_probe.yaml ; configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_contextual_probe.yaml. Artifacts: outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_contextual_probe.md ; outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_contextual_probe.json ; outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_contextual_probe_run_summary.md. Evidence: contextual route-bias core validated and instrumented, but no eval gain; train success stayed 0.000, throughput was 812.3 fps vs 1418.1 for route-bias, and combined route_bias_norm averaged 0.409 vs 0.451, so adding a current-context routing residual kept the bias magnitude but did not improve learning and added substantial overhead.

title: Prototype POR actor-hidden post-FiLM norm probe for Memory conversion

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: partial_shift25 may be working by moving the actor hidden state into a better regime, but the resulting hidden/logit scale is too extreme. A post-FiLM layer norm could preserve the greedy conversion while conditioning the hidden state better; the same mechanism may also rescue the nearby scale325 variant that currently retains only a weak shoulder.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Commands:
- bash -n scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_post_norm_probe.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python -m py_compile src/psmn_rl/config.py src/psmn_rl/models/common.py src/psmn_rl/models/factory.py
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py tests/test_models.py tests/test_training_regressions.py
- bash ./scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_post_norm_probe.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q
Artifacts:
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift25_post_norm.yaml
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift25_scale325_post_norm.yaml
- scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_post_norm_probe.sh
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_post_norm_probe.md
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_post_norm_probe.json
Evidence:
- post_norm on the winning partial_shift25 branch fully collapses greedy/lower/gap/shoulder to 0.0000
- post_norm also fails to rescue the nearby scale325 variant, which also stays at 0.0000 across the full sampled band
- the incumbent partial_shift25 line remains the only live variant with greedy 0.4688 and sampled 0.4688 / 0.4688 / 0.4688
- conclusion: post-FiLM normalization is not the missing conditioning fix for this Memory architecture branch

title: Prototype low-margin top2 rerank Memory architecture probe

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: the Memory branch failure is mostly a low-margin top-choice ordering problem, and broad residual/bias/gain interventions are too disruptive. This probe tests a targeted top-2 reranker that only redistributes score between the current top two actions in low-margin states. Planned matched probe: POR base, POR top2 rerank, GRU base, and GRU top2 rerank on MiniGrid-MemoryS9-v0.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed low-margin top2 rerank Memory architecture probe. Commands: bash -n scripts/run_memory_top2_rerank_probe.sh; python -m py_compile src/psmn_rl/config.py src/psmn_rl/models/common.py src/psmn_rl/models/factory.py; python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py tests/test_models.py tests/test_training_regressions.py; bash ./scripts/run_memory_top2_rerank_probe.sh; python -m pytest -q. Configs: configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_rerun.yaml, configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_top2_rerank.yaml, configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_token_gru_long_rerun.yaml, configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_token_gru_long_top2_rerank.yaml. Artifacts: outputs/reports/memory_top2_rerank_probe.md, outputs/reports/memory_top2_rerank_probe.json, outputs/experiments/memory_top2_rerank_probe/. Evidence: POR top2 rerank collapsed sampled Memory success from 0.3750/0.4531/0.4688 to 0.0000/0.0000/0.0000 at t=0.05/0.055/0.08, and GRU top2 rerank also collapsed from 0.2500/0.3594/0.4531 to 0.0000/0.0000/0.0000. On POR the rerank gate effectively shut off (~3.5e-07), while on GRU it stayed active (~0.214) and still destroyed the sampled band.

- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 256 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.045
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 256 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.0425
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 256 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.045
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 256 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.0425

- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_por_boundary.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_control_variant_thresholds.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_nearby_followup.md
Evidence:
- nearby por_switchy seed7 remains ahead at matched 0.045 over 256 episodes with 0.2617 success versus gru_long_rollout seed11 at 0.1836
- by 0.0425 the gap is already narrow: por_switchy seed7 0.1953 versus gru_long_rollout seed11 0.1836

- the most defensible interpretation is now threshold-shift advantage rather than a large clean architecture separation

- conclusion: duration-conditioned scale softening creates a real sampled-improving but greedy-costly trade-off, not a strict replacement for the incumbent local point

title: Memory POR vs recurrent 1024-episode upper-shoulder replay

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Ran matched 1024-episode upper-shoulder replays on all 4 GPUs for the Memory POR vs recurrent comparison.

Commands:
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.06
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.065
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.06
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.065

Artifacts updated:
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_por_boundary.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_control_variant_thresholds.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_nearby_followup.md

Evidence:
- At 0.06 over 1024 episodes: POR 0.5010 vs GRU 0.4561
- At 0.065 over 1024 episodes: POR 0.5107 vs GRU 0.4844
- Interpretation tightened: the low-0.05x shoulder stays meaningful through 0.06, but by 0.065 the gap is already compressing toward the smaller upper-band margin.

title: Prototype POR duration-gated top2 rerank probe for Memory conversion
close_reason: Restoring closed state. The duration-gated top2 rerank probe is already complete; reopened while scoping the next follow-up.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: the option-aware top2 rerank idea is being suppressed by entropy inside the stability signal, not failing on its own. This probe keeps the same POR option-conditioned top2 rerank but swaps the actor gate from option stability to raw option duration gate, testing whether the Memory sampled band needs a less conservative persistence signal to convert.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented the POR duration-gated top2 rerank probe and validated it end to end. Commands: bash -n scripts/run_memory_option_top2_duration_probe.sh; ./.venv/bin/python -m py_compile src/psmn_rl/config.py src/psmn_rl/models/common.py src/psmn_rl/models/factory.py src/psmn_rl/models/options/por.py; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py tests/test_models.py tests/test_training_regressions.py; bash ./scripts/run_memory_option_top2_duration_probe.sh; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Configs: configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_option_top2_duration.yaml, configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_option_top2_duration_small.yaml. Artifacts: outputs/reports/memory_option_top2_duration_probe.md, outputs/reports/memory_option_top2_duration_probe.json. Result: both duration-gated variants still failed to preserve the live sampled Memory band. The large variant stayed dead at 0.0000 across t=0.05/0.055/0.08. The small variant also stayed 0.0000 across the band despite finally becoming an active intervention: gate signal mean ~0.3025, gate mean ~0.2749, effective delta ~0.0631, and margins collapsing to ~0.049. Useful negative signal: swapping from stability to raw duration gate solved the under-application problem, but the active top2 rerank still did not convert into usable Memory behavior.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: active duration-gated output surgery is still the wrong place to intervene. This probe moves the option-conditioned correction earlier, into the actor hidden representation, using raw duration gate so the option signal can actually fire while avoiding direct logit or top2 manipulation.

title: Predictive base top1 bonus keyed-residual architecture probe
description: Test a bounded architecture variant that preserves the current keyed-residual route-bias path and adds a small bonus on the predictive base-router top expert using a predictive hidden-state projection through the base route head. Goal: see whether one-step predictive state helps at the coarse base-routing layer instead of the keyed residual branch.
close_reason: done: predictive base top1 bonus probe implemented, validated, and rejected; predictive-base disagreement was too rare to matter and underperformed plain keyed residual with no eval gain

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Validated variant sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_top1_bonus. Commands: ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_models.py tests/test_routing.py; bash -n scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_top1_bonus_arch_probe.sh; config load validation for configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_top1_bonus_probe.yaml; bash ./scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_top1_bonus_arch_probe.sh; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Artifacts: outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_top1_bonus_probe.md, outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_top1_bonus_probe.json, outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_top1_bonus_probe_run_summary.md. Evidence: mechanically live predictive-base path with predictive hidden norm 4.997 and predictive base logits norm 0.674, but predictive-base top1 disagreement stayed only 0.004 and predictive base bonus norm stayed 0.0004. Aggregate eval_success stayed 0.000, aggregate best_train_return was only 0.046 vs 0.135 for plain keyed residual, and throughput fell to 1358.0 vs 1432.0. This is a verified negative and shows predictive base-top1 disagreement is too rare to make the coarse-router bonus useful in this family.

title: Predictive-delta mix keyed-residual route-bias architecture probe
description: Probe a predictive-delta mix keyed-residual route-bias variant that keeps the prior-hidden keyed path and adds a fixed small fraction of the predictive keyed delta in logit space. Goal: preserve the strong-seed behavior of the unscaled keyed residual while retaining part of the weak-seed lift from the fully predictive variant on the 24-update DoorKey architecture probe against phase_memory, route_bias, and unscaled keyed_residual.
close_reason: Done. Implemented and verified the predictive-delta mix keyed-residual route-bias probe; focused tests, script syntax, config validation, the 24-update DoorKey probe, and full pytest all passed. The fixed predictive delta was mechanically present but too small to preserve the current keyed-residual lead or the predictive weak-seed lift, so the result is a verified negative.

title: Prototype POR actor-hidden mixed-gate FiLM probe for Memory conversion

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: the first hidden-state FiLM win used raw duration gate, which improved the lower/gap band but slightly softened the shoulder. This probe keeps the actor-hidden FiLM path but mixes duration and stability into the gate so the option-conditioned modulation is still active while damping high-entropy shoulder states.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Commands: bash -n scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_film_mixed_probe.sh; ./.venv/bin/python -m py_compile src/psmn_rl/config.py src/psmn_rl/models/common.py src/psmn_rl/models/factory.py; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py tests/test_models.py tests/test_training_regressions.py; bash ./scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_film_mixed_probe.sh; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Configs: configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_film_mixed50.yaml, configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_film_mixed75.yaml. Artifacts: outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_film_mixed_probe.md, outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_film_mixed_probe.json, outputs/experiments/memory_actor_hidden_film_mixed_probe/. Evidence: same-seed mixed duration/stability gating did not preserve the hidden-FiLM Memory branch. Raw duration hidden-FiLM stayed alive at 0.4375 / 0.4688 / 0.4531 for t=0.05 / 0.055 / 0.08, but mixed50 and mixed75 both collapsed to 0.0000 across the full sampled band while remaining active interventions (gate means 0.0918 and 0.1225, nonzero scale/shift norms). This falsifies the idea that the shoulder tradeoff can be fixed just by mixing stability into the hidden-FiLM gate.

title: Top-keyed weak-base expert gate probe
description: Test a keyed-residual route-bias variant that only applies the weak-base expert boost to the top positively keyed experts, to keep the weak-base rescue signal while reducing broad residual amplification.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_keyed_top2_expert_gate, which applies the weak-base keyed-residual boost only on the top-2 keyed experts with positive keyed logits. Validation: ./\.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_models.py tests/test_routing.py; bash -n scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_keyed_top2_expert_gate_arch_probe.sh; config load validation for the 5 compared probe configs; bash ./scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_keyed_top2_expert_gate_arch_probe.sh; ./\.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Evidence: outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_keyed_top2_expert_gate_probe.md shows aggregate best_train_return 0.058 vs 0.109 for weak_base_expert_gate and 0.135 for plain keyed_residual, greedy eval 0.000, throughput 1263.6. Mechanism finding from the JSON/report: keyed_positive_gate_mean 0.124, keyed_top2_gate_mean 0.500, but combined_weak_keyed_top2_gate_mean only 0.00015 and keyed_residual_scale_mean 1.00008, so the positive-keyed filter mostly shut the boost off. Conclusion: verified negative; top-2 positive keyed gating is too conservative.

title: Capped predictive keyed-residual route-bias architecture probe
description: Probe a capped predictive keyed-residual route-bias variant that computes the keyed residual from a provisional current-step memory state but limits the predictive contribution relative to the prior-hidden keyed path. Goal: keep some of the weak-seed lift from the predictive variant without the strong-seed collapse, on the same 24-update DoorKey architecture probe against phase_memory, route_bias, and unscaled keyed_residual.
close_reason: Done. Implemented and verified the capped predictive keyed-residual route-bias probe; focused tests, script syntax, config validation, the 24-update DoorKey probe, and full pytest all passed. The predictive path remained live but the cap barely activated and aggregate behavior regressed well below the unscaled keyed residual, so the result is a verified negative.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:

- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_models.py tests/test_routing.py

Evidence summary:
- capped predictive variant stayed at greedy eval success 0.000 on both seeds and aggregate best_train_return 0.011 versus 0.135 for unscaled keyed residual

title: Memory POR vs recurrent 1024-episode plateau-bridge replay

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Ran matched 1024-episode shoulder-tail replays on all 4 GPUs for the Memory POR vs recurrent comparison.

Commands:
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.07
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.075
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.07
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.075

Artifacts updated:
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_por_boundary.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_control_variant_thresholds.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_nearby_followup.md

Evidence:
- At 0.07 over 1024 episodes: POR 0.5176 vs GRU 0.4941
- At 0.075 over 1024 episodes: POR 0.5244 vs GRU 0.4922
- Interpretation tightened: by 0.07 the gap is already in the small upper-band range, and 0.075 is effectively on the same plateau as 0.08.

title: Weak-base additive margin bonus architecture probe

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: weak_base_expert_gate remains the only nearby live weak-base lane, while weak_base_margin_advantage failed because the multiplicative scale stayed too close to 1.0. description: Repeat the matched near-threshold POR versus gru_long_rollout comparison on Memory with larger episode counts so the remaining edge can be interpreted more cleanly before planning.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Commands:
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.045
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.0425
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.045
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.0425
Artifact paths:
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_por_boundary.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_control_variant_thresholds.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_nearby_followup.md
Evidence:
- larger-sample replay keeps the same ordering at the matched boundary temperatures
- at 0.045 over 512 episodes, nearby por_switchy seed7 reaches 0.2949 success versus gru_long_rollout seed11 at 0.2207
- at 0.0425 over 512 episodes, nearby por_switchy seed7 reaches 0.2051 versus gru_long_rollout seed11 at 0.1738
- this keeps POR as the strongest nearby branch, but the edge remains modest rather than decisive, which reinforces a threshold-shift interpretation instead of a large clean architecture separation

title: Predictive keyed-residual route-bias architecture probe
description: Probe a predictive keyed-residual route-bias variant that computes the keyed residual from a provisional current-step memory state derived from the current token summary and prior hidden state, while leaving the base route-bias path unchanged. Goal: test whether the current keyed-residual lead is limited by stale memory queries rather than missing logit-side structure, on the same 24-update DoorKey architecture probe against phase_memory, route_bias, and unscaled keyed_residual.
close_reason: Done. Implemented and verified the predictive keyed-residual route-bias probe; focused tests, script syntax, config validation, the 24-update DoorKey probe, and full pytest all passed. The predictive path was mechanically live and improved the weak seed, but aggregate behavior still trailed the unscaled keyed residual, so the result is a mixed but non-winning architecture variant.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Commands:
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_models.py tests/test_routing.py

- predictive variant stayed at greedy eval success 0.000 on both seeds
- aggregate best_train_return reached 0.111 versus 0.135 for unscaled keyed residual in the run summary
- predictive querying was mechanically live (predictive_hidden_norm 4.463, keyed_predictive_delta_norm 1.513) and improved the weak seed to best_train_return 0.156, but it cut the strong seed to 0.066, so it is a mixed live result rather than a new lead

- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.055
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.0525
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 1024 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.055

- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_por_boundary.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_control_variant_thresholds.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_nearby_followup.md

Evidence:
- At 0.0525 over 1024 episodes: POR 0.4424 vs GRU 0.3564
- At 0.055 over 1024 episodes: POR 0.4775 vs GRU 0.4062
- Interpretation tightened: the strongest separation still centers on 0.05, but 0.0525 and 0.055 form a real low-0.05x shoulder rather than collapsing immediately toward the smaller 0.08 gap.

comments.text:

- bash -n scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_adaptive_shift_comp_probe.sh

- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_adaptive_shift_comp_probe.md
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_adaptive_shift_comp_probe.json

Evidence:
- adaptive_floor25 baseline stayed at greedy/lower/gap/shoulder 0.3125 / 0.4688 / 0.4844 / 0.4688
- shiftcomp50 improved sampled lower/gap/shoulder to 0.5312 / 0.5312 / 0.5469 but greedy collapsed to 0.0000
- shiftcomp100 collapsed completely to 0.0000 across greedy and sampled modes
- conclusion: compensating the additive hidden shift does not improve the adaptive-floor trade-off; it produces another sampled-only branch or a full collapse

title: Bounded tokenized-control recovery sweep

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Launching bounded DoorKey entropy sweep on tokenized controls only. Runs: token_dense ent_coef in {1e-3, 0.0} and single_expert ent_coef in {1e-3, 0.0}. This is directly justified by the policy-extraction result: single_expert already solves under sampled eval but fails under greedy, while token_dense remains soft and underconfident. Goal: recover at least one greedy-competent tokenized control before any routed retest.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Completed bounded tokenized-control recovery sweep. The smallest successful intervention was lowering token_dense PPO ent_coef from 0.01 to 0.001, which recovered greedy DoorKey success to 0.75 and improved representation health. token_dense ent_coef=0.0 and both single_expert entropy variants did not recover a greedy-competent control.

title: Inspect DoorKey tokenized-control bottlenecks in current code
close_reason: Inspection complete; ranked likely tokenized-control bottlenecks in current DoorKey code with exact model/train/eval/config references and minimal follow-up interventions.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Inspecting current DoorKey control code paths to rank likely bottlenecks in tokenized vs flat_dense performance, with focus on obs encoding, pooling, mixing depth, actor/value inputs, and greedy eval behavior.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Findings: strongest shared bottlenecks appear to be token-level mean pooling of all MiniGrid cells plus a lightly weighted direction token, actor/value heads consuming only pooled features, and no cross-token mixing at all in single_expert/SARE beyond pooling. Train always samples while eval defaults to greedy argmax, so sampled eval should be checked before concluding policy learning is absent.

Evidence summary:
- The combined gate became too conservative: weak_base_gate_mean 0.419, weak_keyed_gate_mean 0.414, but combined_gate_mean only 0.124.
- Behavior regressed strongly: aggregate greedy eval stayed 0.000 and best_train_return fell to 0.027 versus 0.135 for plain keyed residual and 0.109 for the prior weak-base expert gate.
- Throughput also fell to 1334.7 versus 1410.2 for plain keyed residual, so the stricter gate lost both behavior and efficiency.

title: Weak-prior predictive assist keyed-residual architecture probe

Goal: preserve the current strong keyed-residual regime while giving weak-prior experts more predictive routing headroom.
close_reason: done: weak-prior predictive assist probe implemented and validated; mechanically live but it reduced route-bias strength and underperformed the keyed residual baseline

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented and validated the weak-prior predictive assist keyed-residual route-bias probe. Commands: ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_models.py tests/test_routing.py ; bash -n scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_weak_prior_assist_arch_probe.sh ; config load validation for configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_weak_prior_assist_probe.yaml ; bash ./scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_weak_prior_assist_arch_probe.sh ; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Config: configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_weak_prior_assist_probe.yaml. Artifacts: outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_weak_prior_assist_probe.md ; outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_weak_prior_assist_probe.json ; outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_weak_prior_assist_probe_run_summary.md ; outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_weak_prior_assist_probe_run_summary.csv. Evidence: assist path was mechanically live with weak_prior_assist_gate=0.341, predictive_hidden_norm=4.354, and assisted_predictive_keyed_route_bias_logits_norm=0.024. But aggregate best_train_return fell to 0.014 vs 0.135 for sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual, greedy eval stayed 0.000, route entropy rose to 1.382, and throughput fell to 1357.3 vs 1407.1. This is not an architecture improvement.

- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 64 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.045

Artifact paths:
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_por_boundary.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_nearby_followup.md
Evidence:
- 0.05 remains genuinely alive on longer repeats: 0.3438 over 128 episodes and 0.3711 over 256
- 0.045 is still meaningfully alive at 0.2656
- 0.04 is sharply degraded but still nonzero, including 0.1367 over 256 episodes

title: Concentration-gated weak-base expert blend architecture probe
description: Test a weak-base expert-gated keyed-residual variant that blends between plain keyed residual and weak-base boosted keyed residual based on per-sample weak-base gate concentration, then validate it with the standard DoorKey architecture probe and full verification path.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_expert_concentration_blend, added config/runner/tests, ran focused pytest, shell syntax, config load validation, the 2-seed 5-config DoorKey probe, a matched 64-episode greedy+sampled comparison against weak_base_expert_gate and keyed_residual, and full pytest. Evidence: outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_expert_concentration_blend_probe.md plus the matching .json and _run_summary.md, and outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_expert_concentration_blend_sampled_eval.md. Result: no verified improvement. The short probe tied weak_base_expert_gate on aggregate best_train_return at 0.109, but the concentration scalar saturated to 1.0, throughput was lower at 1354.1 vs 1366.0, and the current matched sampled rerun was flat at 0.0 success / 0.0 return for the new variant, weak_base_expert_gate, and plain keyed_residual. That makes this effectively a near-identity rewrite of weak_base_expert_gate under current checkpoints, not a stronger architecture.

title: Prototype POR actor-hidden low-margin FiLM gate probe for Memory conversion

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: the current partial_shift25 winner may be over-applying the hidden-FiLM intervention. If the FiLM gate is multiplied by a base-policy low-margin gate, the intervention may stay concentrated on ambiguous decisions, preserve the new greedy conversion, and reduce the saturation/shoulder tradeoff.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Commands:
- bash -n scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_low_margin_probe.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python -m py_compile src/psmn_rl/config.py src/psmn_rl/models/common.py src/psmn_rl/models/factory.py
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py tests/test_models.py tests/test_training_regressions.py
- bash ./scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_low_margin_probe.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q
Artifacts:
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift25_low_margin35.yaml
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift25_low_margin50.yaml
- scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_low_margin_probe.sh
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_low_margin_probe.md
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_low_margin_probe.json
Evidence:
- both low-margin-gated variants collapse to greedy/lower/gap/shoulder 0.0000
- the added base-policy margin gate effectively shuts the FiLM path off: low_margin_gate_mean falls to about 2.7e-4 and 3.5e-5, with scale/shift norms near zero
- this shows the current winner is not over-applying on low-margin states; instead its own pre-FiLM policy is already high-margin enough that a base-margin gate disables the intervention entirely
- the incumbent partial_shift25 line remains the only live variant with greedy 0.4688 and sampled 0.4688 / 0.4688 / 0.4688

title: Abs-top2 weak-base expert gate probe
description: Test a keyed-residual route-bias variant that applies the weak-base expert boost only to the top-2 experts by keyed-logit magnitude within the weak-base subset, to reduce broad residual amplification without the over-suppression from positive-only or sign-aligned filters.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_keyed_abs_top2_expert_gate, which applies the weak-base keyed-residual boost only to the top-2 experts by keyed-logit magnitude. Validation: ./\.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_models.py tests/test_routing.py; bash -n scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_keyed_abs_top2_expert_gate_arch_probe.sh; config load validation for the 5 compared probe configs; bash ./scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_keyed_abs_top2_expert_gate_arch_probe.sh; sampled follow-up via source .venv/bin/activate && PSMN_DEVICE=auto bash ./scripts/eval_policy_modes.sh on best abs-top2 run plus keyed_residual and weak_base_expert_gate references over 64 episodes; ./\.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Evidence: outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_keyed_abs_top2_expert_gate_probe.md shows aggregate best_train_return 0.100 vs 0.109 for weak_base_expert_gate and 0.135 for plain keyed_residual, greedy eval 0.000, throughput 1344.2. Mechanism finding: keyed_abs_top2_gate_mean 0.500, combined_weak_keyed_abs_top2_gate_mean 0.263, keyed_residual_scale_mean 1.131, so the gate stayed live on both seeds instead of collapsing. But outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_weak_base_keyed_abs_top2_expert_gate_sampled_eval.md shows sampled success only 0.031250 on the best abs-top2 checkpoint versus 0.343750 for keyed_residual and 0.484375 for weak_base_expert_gate. Conclusion: verified negative; abs-top2 gating is more balanced in train metrics but substantially worse in sampled policy quality.

title: Prototype POR option-aware low-margin adapter probe for Memory conversion
close_reason: Done. Implemented and verified the POR option-aware low-margin adapter Memory probe; the gate stayed nearly off and both variants failed to preserve the sampled band.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: the Memory branch is failing from overconfident decode, not missing option-state signal. This probe exports a POR option-aware feature and applies a learned correction only in low-margin actor states, so the base policy remains unchanged unless both persistence stability and decode ambiguity are present.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Implemented the POR option-aware low-margin adapter probe and validated it end to end. Commands: bash -n scripts/run_memory_option_margin_adapter_probe.sh; ./.venv/bin/python -m py_compile src/psmn_rl/config.py src/psmn_rl/models/common.py src/psmn_rl/models/factory.py src/psmn_rl/models/options/por.py; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py tests/test_models.py tests/test_training_regressions.py; bash ./scripts/run_memory_option_margin_adapter_probe.sh; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Configs: configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_option_margin_adapter.yaml, configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_option_margin_adapter_small.yaml. Artifacts: outputs/reports/memory_option_margin_adapter_probe.md, outputs/reports/memory_option_margin_adapter_probe.json. Result: both option-margin variants failed to preserve the live sampled Memory band. The large variant collapsed to 0.0000 at t=0.05/0.055/0.08, and the small variant only reached 0.0156 at t=0.08. Useful negative signal: the low-margin actor gate barely activated (gate mean ~1.7e-05 and 5.5e-05), so this probe died from under-application rather than from destructive overconfidence.

Existing data already includes seed-29 KL-LSS SARE/token_dense summaries, round datasets/checkpoints, and extended route-dependence report rows; gap is that route probes are single-draw aggregate evals with no saved traces and SARE path_entropy is top-1-only. Repeated current randomization averages 0.9785 greedy success, stronger random-single-expert averages 0.9473, while every fixed-pair router override collapses to 0.0 and most expert ablations collapse too. title: Memory recurrent calibration-shift replay
description: Replay the strongest recurrent Memory control at slightly higher low temperatures so we can tell whether the remaining POR edge is mostly a calibration shift or a real margin before more planning.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Commands:
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.05
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.0525
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.055
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.06
Artifact paths:
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_control_variant_thresholds.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_nearby_followup.md
Evidence:
- gru_long_rollout seed11 improves materially with a small upward temperature shift: 0.2988 at 0.05, 0.3379 at 0.0525, 0.4023 at 0.055, and 0.4434 at 0.06 over 512 episodes
- that means the strongest recurrent control can nearly match or exceed the best POR boundary results with only a modest calibration shift
- the remaining POR advantage therefore looks mostly like a left-shifted temperature threshold rather than a broad architecture-only separation
- the control still collapses below 0.03, so the low-temperature tail shape is not identical even though the higher part of the band can be matched

comments.text:

- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 256 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.03
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 256 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.025
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 256 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.02
Artifact paths:
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_control_variant_thresholds.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_nearby_followup.md
Evidence:
- gru_long_rollout seed11 stays meaningfully alive at 0.04 over 256 episodes with 0.1211 success
- the same checkpoint weakens to 0.0117 at 0.03, 0.0039 at 0.025, and 0.0078 at 0.02 over 256 episodes
- this validates that the Memory control-side low-temperature effect is real through 0.04 but mostly collapses below 0.03
- the best nearby POR checkpoint still has the stronger high-success narrow band at 0.1 and 0.05, so POR remains the strongest nearby branch even though the phenomenon is not POR-only

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: partial_shift25 may be above the minimum shift needed for greedy conversion. A lower shift weight could preserve the new greedy lift while softening the small shoulder regression and reducing the extreme margin saturation.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Commands:
- bash -n scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_lower_shift_probe.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py
- bash ./scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_lower_shift_probe.sh
- 4-GPU 256-episode confirmation eval on por_base / por_actor_hidden_partial_shift20 / por_actor_hidden_partial_shift25 across greedy, t=0.05, t=0.055, t=0.08
Artifacts:
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift15.yaml
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift20.yaml
- scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_lower_shift_probe.sh
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_lower_shift_probe.md
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_lower_shift_probe.json
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_lower_shift_confirmation.md
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_lower_shift_confirmation.json
Evidence:
- partial_shift15 fully collapses despite train success 0.55, so the new greedy conversion is not robust below the shift threshold
- partial_shift20 is sampled-alive and improves the sampled band over base and over partial_shift25 by +0.0312 / +0.0195 / +0.0078 on the 256-episode confirmation, but greedy stays 0.0000
- partial_shift25 remains the best architecture result because it keeps greedy 0.4688 over 256 episodes while partial_shift20 cannot preserve greedy conversion
- conclusion: smaller shift weights do not beat the existing partial_shift25 result

title: Predictive base delta entropy-gated keyed-residual architecture probe
description: Test a bounded architecture variant that preserves the current keyed-residual route-bias path and adds the predictive base-route delta only in proportion to normalized base-router uncertainty. Goal: keep the predictive-base path active where the router is uncertain while avoiding the uniform full-vector correction that underperformed in the plain delta-mix probe.
close_reason: done: predictive base delta entropy-gated probe implemented, validated, and rejected; the gate stayed near-on and still underperformed plain keyed residual with no eval gain

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Validated variant sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_entropy_gate. Commands: ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_models.py tests/test_routing.py; bash -n scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_entropy_gate_arch_probe.sh; config load validation for configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_entropy_gate_probe.yaml; bash ./scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_entropy_gate_arch_probe.sh; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Artifacts: outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_entropy_gate_probe.md, outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_entropy_gate_probe.json, outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_entropy_gate_probe_run_summary.md. Evidence: entropy-gated predictive-base path stayed mechanically active with predictive base logits norm 0.813, predictive base delta norm 0.093, mixed predictive base delta norm 0.021, uncertainty gate 0.925, and predictive hidden norm 4.914. But aggregate eval_success stayed 0.000, aggregate best_train_return was only 0.009 vs 0.135 for plain keyed residual, and throughput fell to 1369.9 vs 1438.7. This is a verified negative: entropy gating did not meaningfully suppress the predictive-base correction and still underperformed the plain keyed-residual core.

title: Blended predictive keyed-residual route-bias architecture probe
description: Probe a blended predictive keyed-residual route-bias variant that computes the keyed residual from a fixed blend of prior hidden state and provisional current-step memory, while leaving the base route-bias path unchanged. Goal: preserve the strong-seed behavior of the current keyed-residual lead while retaining some of the weak-seed lift seen in the fully predictive variant on the 24-update DoorKey architecture probe against phase_memory, route_bias, and unscaled keyed_residual.
close_reason: Done. Implemented and verified the blended predictive keyed-residual route-bias probe; focused tests, script syntax, config validation, a clean rerun of the 24-update DoorKey probe, and full pytest all passed. The blended path was mechanically live after a mixed-precision fix, but aggregate behavior regressed well below the unscaled keyed residual, so the result is a verified negative.

title: Calibrate POR actor-hidden adaptive shift compensation on Memory
description: Run a small calibration sweep on the existing adaptive shift-compensation surface to test whether milder compensation than 0.5 can improve the adaptive_floor25 sampled band without collapsing greedy on the live Memory branch.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Commands:
- bash -n scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_adaptive_shift_comp_calibration.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py
- bash ./scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_adaptive_shift_comp_calibration.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q

Configs:
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift22_adaptive_floor25_shiftcomp20.yaml
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift22_adaptive_floor25_shiftcomp35.yaml

Artifacts:
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_adaptive_shift_comp_calibration.md
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_adaptive_shift_comp_calibration.json

Evidence:
- adaptive_floor25 baseline stayed at greedy/lower/gap/shoulder 0.3125 / 0.4688 / 0.4844 / 0.4688
- shiftcomp20 collapsed to 0.0000 across greedy and sampled modes
- shiftcomp35 collapsed to 0.0000 across greedy and sampled modes
- only the already-known shiftcomp50 point remained sampled-only at 0.5312 / 0.5312 / 0.5469 with greedy 0.0000
- conclusion: no interior compensation point improves the adaptive-floor trade-off enough to justify a confirmation pass

title: Memory POR vs recurrent interpolation replay
description: Replay the best nearby POR checkpoint and the strongest recurrent control at 0.0475 and 0.04 on Memory with larger episode counts so the remaining edge can be mapped just above and just below the current boundary band before planning.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Commands:
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.0475
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_nearby_followup/por_stage2_alive_baseline/memory/por_switchy/seed_7/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.04
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.0475
- CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 ./.venv/bin/python -m psmn_rl.evaluate --config outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/generated_config.yaml --checkpoint outputs/experiments/lss_next_arch_wave_program/por_stage1_recurrent_controls/memory/gru_long_rollout/seed_11/latest.pt --episodes 512 --device auto --greedy false --temperature 0.04
Artifact paths:
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_por_boundary.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_memory_control_variant_thresholds.md
- outputs/reports/next_arch_wave_nearby_followup.md
Evidence:
- at 0.0475 over 512 episodes, nearby por_switchy seed7 reaches 0.3457 success versus gru_long_rollout seed11 at 0.2520
- at 0.04 over 512 episodes, nearby por_switchy seed7 reaches 0.1738 versus gru_long_rollout seed11 at 0.1309
- this means the POR edge widens somewhat just above the current boundary band, then narrows again by 0.04
- the best nearby interpretation remains a modest threshold-shift advantage, not a broad clean architecture separation

close_reason: Done. Implemented and verified the EMA keyed-residual route-bias probe; focused tests, script syntax, config validation, the 24-update DoorKey probe, and full pytest all passed. The EMA memory path was live but reduced the current keyed-residual lead instead of stabilizing it, so the result is a verified negative.

comments.text:

- bash -n scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_blend_probe.sh

Evidence:
- Both learned blend variants destroyed the partial_shift25 win: greedy 0.0000 and sampled 0.0000 across t=0.05/0.055/0.08, while ungated partial_shift25 held 0.4688 on greedy and all sampled bands.
- Blend gates were active (mean 0.8818 and 1.0000 after scaling/clamp), so this falsifies the hypothesis that a learned fallback blend would preserve or improve the live Memory branch.

title: Prototype low-margin logit-gain Memory architecture probe

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Hypothesis: the Memory branch failure is a decode/selection problem, and additive residuals are too disruptive. title: Predictive base delta-mix keyed-residual architecture probe
description: Test a bounded architecture variant that preserves the current keyed-residual route-bias path and adds a small full-vector residual from predictive base-route logits relative to base-route logits. Goal: see whether predictive state helps as a coarse routing correction even when top1 disagreement triggers are too sparse.
close_reason: done: predictive base delta-mix probe implemented, validated, and rejected; predictive-base correction was active but still underperformed plain keyed residual with no eval gain

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text: Validated variant sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_mix. Commands: ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_models.py tests/test_routing.py; bash -n scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_mix_arch_probe.sh; config load validation for configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_probe.yaml configs/diagnostic/minigrid_doorkey_sare_phase_memory_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_mix_probe.yaml; bash ./scripts/run_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_mix_arch_probe.sh; ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q. Artifacts: outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_mix_probe.md, outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_mix_probe.json, outputs/reports/architecture_route_bias_keyed_residual_predictive_base_delta_mix_probe_run_summary.md. Evidence: predictive-base delta path is mechanically active with predictive base logits norm 0.779, predictive base delta norm 0.094, mixed predictive base delta norm 0.024, and predictive hidden norm 4.907. But aggregate eval_success stayed 0.000, aggregate best_train_return was only 0.025 vs 0.135 for plain keyed residual, and throughput fell to 1392.4 vs 1414.9. This is a verified negative: a full-vector predictive base correction is more active than the sparse trigger variants, but it still does not beat the current keyed-residual core.

title: Probe shift-weight refinement around shiftgate075 for Memory conversion
description: Test whether slightly retuning hidden shift weight around the confirmed partial_shift22+shiftgate075 local win improves greedy/sample performance on Memory. Add minimal configs/runner/test surface, run a fresh 4-GPU probe, and run a 256-episode confirmation pass if a variant improves on shiftgate075.

comments.author: Chris Taylor
comments.text:
Completed shift-weight refinement probe around shiftgate075 for Memory conversion.

Commands run:
- bash -n scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_shiftgate075_shift_weight_probe.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python - <<'PY' ... load_config checks for shift215_shiftgate075/shift225_shiftgate075 ... PY
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_configs.py tests/test_models.py tests/test_training_regressions.py
- bash ./scripts/run_memory_actor_hidden_shiftgate075_shift_weight_probe.sh
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q

Configs:
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift215_shiftgate075.yaml
- configs/experiments/minigrid_memory_por_switchy_actor_hidden_partial_shift225_shiftgate075.yaml

Artifacts:
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_shiftgate075_shift_weight_probe.md
- outputs/reports/memory_actor_hidden_shiftgate075_shift_weight_probe.json
- outputs/experiments/memory_actor_hidden_shiftgate075_shift_weight_probe/

Evidence summary:
- shift215_shiftgate075 collapsed to 0.0000 / 0.0000 / 0.0000 / 0.0000 on greedy / t=0.05 / t=0.055 / t=0.08
- shift225_shiftgate075 tied the incumbent exactly at 0.4688 / 0.4688 / 0.4688 / 0.4688
- the shiftgate075 incumbent remained the best-known point on this local surface; retuning shift weight around it did not improve the restored plateau.
